arts.ttl: Venezia vs. Genoa: A battle to escape the bottom
arts.sum: Venezia have drawn each of their last three Serie A matches against Genoa; it’s their longest ongoing draw streak for them in the competition. Genoa have scored two goals in the last 15 minutes of matches this season, fewer than only Napoli (4), while Venezia are one of the two teams that have conceded the most goals in the first half of the game (6, level with Fiorentina).

arts.ttl: Serie A | Venezia v Genoa: Match Preview
arts.sum: Struggling Venezia host mid-table Genoa at the Stadio Pier Luigi Penzo on Saturday as they search for their first win since returning to Serie A this season. The newly-promoted Lagunari sit rock-bottom of the early standings with just one point from four matches, while their visitors have made a solid start with five points so far. After gaining promotion via the Serie B playoffs last season, Venezia have found life tough back in the top flight under new manager Eusebio Di Francesco. arts.ttl: Gollini: “I’d like to stay with Genoa for a long time”
arts.sum: Genoa goalkeeper Pierluigi Gollini expressed his desire to remain with the club long-term during the opening of the team’s pavilion at the Genoa Boat Show. “I would really like to stay with Genoa for a long time,” Gollini stated. The goalkeeper spoke about the club and his future at the event. Reflecting on Genoa’s start to the Serie A season, which has seen two draws, one loss, and one win in the first four matches, Gollini said, “Our goal is to always take the field and give our best.
